question: Which, of the items listed below, is NOT a type of Amateur licence?

You are operating in the 'all modes' section of the 2 metre band plan, sending CW with the 'mode' (type of transmission) switch set to CW. You then switch mode to FM to talk to your Morse practice tutor. Before discussing your Morse sending you should
options: say that you have changed mode.

question: An amateur is not operating in accordance with the licence if they
options: fail to give the call sign of the person being spoken to on every over.
transmit information intended for general reception.
transmit to a group of more than five amateurs.

question: An authorised person from which organisation has the right to require modification of the Radio Equipment?
options: The Radio Society of Great Britain.
The Institute of Engineering and Technology.

question: For the purposes of the EMF requirements in the amateur licence the description "General Public" means
options: everybody apart from the licence holder in person.
everybody except those living at the amateur's house.
people outside the amateur's circle of family and friends.
people outside the amateur's family and relatives.

question: When in London, the band 431.0-432.0MHz is
The Volt is a unit of
Alternating current is
options: a current which periodically changes in direction.

question: The key feature of a digital signal, when compared to an analogue signal is that
options: the change between the amplitude of the signal at one instant in time to another is smooth and gradual.
the maximum amplitude of the signal is not limited as a result of digital processing.
a digital signal can represent an analogue signal that has been sampled at regular intervals.
It is not possible to process a digital signal using software applications and techniques.

A radio amateur decides to erect a second dipole of the same type but a different length. This is done because
options: the second antenna will be kept as a spare.
the second antenna is intended to be used on a different frequency.
the amateur wants to talk to two friends at the same time.
two antennas will give a stronger signal than one.

question: A mobile VHF radio receiver is fitted in a car. The car is driven along a clear open road directly away from the transmitter but is always in sight of the transmitter. The received signal will
options: remain a constant strength or level all the time the transmitter is in sight.
vanish once the transmit aerial is too small to be seen.
get stronger as the distance increases.
get weaker as the distance increases.

question: Ducting is a propagation effect that
options: extends the range of HF signals.
reduces the range of VHF and UHF signals.
extends the range of VHF and UHF signals.
reduces the range of HF signals.

question: The avoidance of interference between two items of electrical equipment is known as

A case of interference is proving difficult to solve. Before calling Ofcom, who else may be able to help you?
Before making an initial call a radio amateur should
options: make an entry in the log book.

question: Band Plans are published because
options: it allows the different modes and types of transmission to best share the band.
it is a convenient way of remembering the schedule to the amateur licence.
it is illegal to operate in contravention of the band plan.
it allocates most of the band to the higher power stations.

question: Connecting anything to the Microphone socket of a transmitter other than the supplied microphone may
options: cause incorrect operation of the PTT.
cause changes in the received signal.

question: High voltages may cause
A risk when drilling metal is
options: the centre punch will mark the work.
swarf will fly up towards the eyes.
the drill bit will become blunt.

question: The main detrimental effect on health of being too close to a high power transmission is
If you move house and want to transmit from your new home, you must
options: give immediate notice of the new address to Ofcom.
notify the local police by letter.
inform RSGB headquarters by e-mail or phone.

question: The Licensees callsign must be transmitted
options: when another Station replies to a "CQ" call.
when the entry is made in the log book.

question: Before joining your regular club net from your main station address, you must first
options: ask if the frequency is clear.
give your location using either your address or other geographical description correct to 5 km.
give your location using either your full postcode or the IARU locator to 6 characters.
establish communication with at least one amateur in the net.

question: Foundation licence holder may be instructed to keep a log of transmissions for a period by
The lowest radiated power to which the EMF requirements apply is
When close to London, operating on a frequency of 431.5MHz is
options: limited to a transmit power of 10W erp.
only allowed from fixed stations, no mobile operation.
permitted using Morse and telephony only.

question: If two resistors of different values are connected in parallel and a potential difference is applied across them, the potential difference will be
options: highest across the resistor with the highest value.
equal to the total resistance divided by the potential difference applied.
the sum of the potential difference across each resistor.

question: Which one of the following bands is known as VHF spectrum?
An Analogue to Digital convertor (ADC)
options: Is an optional component in a Software Defined Radio Receiver.
Samples an analogue signal and creates a digital representation of it.
Creates a digital signal in analogue format.

question: A transmission is heard using a few audio tones sent in succession. It is likely to be
The oscillator stage of a transmitter
options: will cause interference unless built in a plastic enclosure or box.
is only needed if the user wishes to send Morse code.
determines the tone of the audio signal.

question: In the block diagram of a radio receiver shown, what is the function of the box marked '2'?
The proportion of the radio signal lost in a feeder will
options: stay much the same at any frequency.
depend on the type of modulation.

question: The polarisation of the radio wave from the antenna shown is
Which one of the following should be used to indicate the reflected power in a feeder to an antenna?
The troposphere
options: is the region above the ionosphere.
is the region below the ionosphere.
is ionised only during the day.

question: Attenuation of UHF signals can be caused by
The ability of a piece of equipment to withstand a level of interference is known as
An amateur transmitter is designed to radiate from the antenna. How might signals enter other electrical devices in the house?
options: By being picked up by mains wires leading to the affected device.
By direct radiation out of the front panel of the transmitter.
By being conducted along the microphone lead.
By radiating from the Morse key.

question: If a neighbour complains of interference from your station you should first
options: keep a log of your transmissions and ask your neighbour to log instances of interference.
ask the neighbour to demonstrate the problem.
check that you have been operating in accordance with the licence Schedule.
check that your licence has not expired.

question: When calling CQ on a VHF FM calling channel, a radio amateur should
options: give the call sign 2 or 3 times and say "CQ" when somebody replies.
say "CQ" 2 or 3 times and give the call sign, then listen for a reply.
say "CQ" a number of times and give the call sign when a contact is established.
say "CQ" about 20 times and listen for a reply.

The correct connections for a 3 core cable to a UK mains plug are
options: blue to N, brown to L, green/yellow to E.
brown to L, blue to E, green/yellow to N.
brown to N, blue to L, green/yellow to E.
blue to L, brown to E, yellow/green to N.

question: When not being used, a hot soldering iron should be
options: put in a well ventilated room.

question: The ICNIRP is the body which advises on
Which item below is a type of UK amateur licence?
The Station callsign must be transmitted
options: when the entry is made in the log book.
at the end of each transmission.
every half hour and when the Station is closing down.
at the beginning of a transmission on a new frequency.

question: Which of the following messages is NOT permitted by the Foundation licence?
options: Agreeing to meet at the radio club.
A secret message to a radio amateur friend.

question: When MUST a Foundation licensee keep a log of transmissions?
options: When in another Region of the UK.
When a contest is being held in the same frequency band as you are operating.
When operating from a Temporary Location.
When instructed to do so by an Ofcom official.

question: An amateur determines that the station is compliant with the EMF requirements set out in the amateur licence. What must then be done?
options: Enter the date of the compliance check in the station log.
Keep a record showing how the station is compliant.
Send a copy of the relevant calculations to Ofcom.
Send a copy of the relevant calculations to the RSGB.

question: The highest frequency allocated to Foundation Licensees is
In a resistor there is a transfer of energy from
In which amateur band are frequencies between 28.225 and 28.300MHz?
Analogue signals
options: Are a stream of finite values at a specific sampling interval.
Are constantly changing in amplitude, frequency or both.
Are produced by an analogue to digital converter.
Must be processed by a computing device with suitable software.

question: Amplitude modulation (AM) produces
options: a radio frequency signal which is changing in frequency.
a single sideband with no carrier.
two sidebands and a full carrier.
a single sideband with a full carrier.

question: The microphone amplifier in an analogue amateur radio transmitter limits the audio frequencies to the range
In which block of the radio receiver shown is the audio modulation recovered?
A balun is
options: used instead of a dummy load.
used to connect a dipole to coaxial cable.

question: A particular yagi antenna has a gain of 3 times. Which transmitter will result in an ERP of 60W?
A radio amateur has a single wire antenna for use on all the HF bands. What should be used to ensure the antenna can accept RF power from the transmitter on any of the bands?
The ionosphere is
options: a type of spherical transmitting antenna.
another name for the air we breathe.
a piece of amateur radio test equipment.
conductive gasses at heights of 70 to 400km.

question: Where should a radio amateur place the antenna to achieve the best range?
options: On a tripod in the garden.
On the side of the house away from the distant Station.

question: The audio from a medium wave broadcast receiver sounds distorted when an amateur transmitter is in use. A likely reason for this is
options: the signal from the amateur transmitter is causing interference.
that the electricity supply cannot power transmitters and receivers at the same time.
the transmitter is operating on the wrong sideband.
the broadcast receiver is not tuned in to the amateur transmitter.

question: What is the purpose of a ferrite ring on the mains lead of an equipment found likely to suffer from interference?
options: The ferrite ring will ensure the interference on each of the mains wires is equal.
The ferrite ring will avoid the need for the equipment to be earthed.
The ferrite ring will minimise unwanted signals leaking out of the affected equipment.
The ferrite ring will minimise any RF on the mains lead from entering the equipment.

question: Which organisation produces guidance on dealing with cases of interference?
options: The Radio Society of Great Britain.

question: The reason for having a calling channel is to
options: provide a known frequency where contacts may be established.

question: During a conversation via a UHF amateur repeater it is noted that after a couple of minutes a beeping tone is heard on the audio of the repeater. It is likely that this tone
options: indicates a malfunction in the repeater.
is a time-out tone added to overly long 'overs'.
is due to a beat note between the input and output audio signals.
indicates the need for CTCSS access to the repeater.

question: Which of the following frequencies should be avoided for terrestrial contacts?
An important difference between a fuse and an RCBO (Residual current Circuit Breaker with Overload protection) is that
options: a fuse protects against excessive currents but an RCBO will also detect small currents to earth.
a fuse will blow if the live wire is touched but an RCBO is unlikely to respond to that event.
an RCBO does not provide all the protection of a properly fitted and rated fuse.
an RCBO must not be used if there is a fuse in the plug of any appliances that will be used.

question: When climbing a ladder to erect an antenna
options: maintain a good electrical connection with earth using a length of wire.
choose a very dry day to reduce the effects of static charge.
always use the help of an assistant to hold the ladder.
always use a wooden ladder to avoid electric shock.

question: It is dangerous to touch a transmitting antenna because
options: a shock or burn may occur.
damage may be caused to the transmitter.
the signal will not be transmitted properly.
